key_points: Towers dorms (Wilson/Wallace) structurally suspect, students joke they sway
Friley has hidden 'Narnia stairs' and abandoned offices worth exploring
Older dorms like Storms were imploded; Friley recently renovated
key_quotes: I used to live in Wallace and have had this exact same dream while living there. I swear those buildings sway
Fortunately, in reality Wallace will probably tip over first
Some of us remember when Knapp and Storm were imploded

key_points: Campus culture is warm, quirky, and genuinely community-driven
KQ scavenger hunt, First Amendment Days, protests show real student engagement
Cult recruiters actively approach students in the library — real documented issue
key_quotes: CyRide is a place to cry. During finals it can also be known as CryRide
I met cult recruiters in the library... they were Moonies
Jeff's is 6.2/10 sober and 9.5/10 smashed

key_points: Campus C-stores massively overprice basic items vs. Walmart
Women's deodorant $4 more than men's in C-store — pink tax real
Hickory Park (local staple) widely considered mediocre since ownership change
key_quotes: That degree deodorant is only $3.97 at Walmart, and the C-store is artificially inflating the price
Iowa State bookstore, the biggest scam on campus
Hickory Park is great for old people who can't handle seasoning in their food

key_points: Students crowdsource crying spots — roommate overcrowding is a real stressor
Dr. Butler's calculus videos cited as more emotionally helpful than therapy
ISU has formal support resources but students still feel resource-starved
key_quotes: He's mentally helped me more times than my therapist. Sure my therapist helped me with my father abuse, but math is so much more stressful
i seriously have NOWHERE to cry. my roommate is home all the time
College can definitely be tough for many people. Please make sure you are taking the proper steps

key_points: ISU offers strong merit scholarships — OOS students report full-tuition awards
Multiple students chose ISU explicitly to avoid six-figure debt
No specific complaints about financial aid office processes in the data
key_quotes: They are covering my entire cost of tuition and I will save over 100K in my college fund over the course of 4 years
ISU is the better option. They've offered me more money, have a better engineering program
Go to Iowa and exit with next to no student debt or go to a T20 and exit with 200k+ debt

key_points: Exam questions routinely look nothing like sample exams — widespread complaint
Dr. Steve Butler's free calculus YouTube videos are a campus legend and lifesaver
AI cheating with Meta smart glasses openly happening in calc classes
key_quotes: One came up bragging to me after the last exam about how they got an A with their meta glasses
Only reason I passed Calc 2 the third time. the goat
Typical day in the computer science department — exams that look nothing like the sample ones

key_points: Student government president impeachment dismissed under political pressure — accountability gap
Iowa legislature actively restricting DEI, faculty speech, and international hiring at ISU
University switched to Workday system — students and staff universally hate it
key_quotes: The first amendment does not apply to a student government impeachment — this is a direct result of Brandt appointing his own justices
Senate File 2435 forbids public universities from teaching about inequities against minority and underserved populations
With my last breath I curse Workday and everyone who was bribed to let it replace A+

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — Campanile and Four Seasons fountain beloved by alumni
Midwest politeness is real and consistent — multiple students independently confirm
Ames is small; Welch Ave bar scene shrinking, CVS became a Rally House
key_quotes: Whenever I go out or bike around, people greet me warmly. I hear 'thank you,' 'sorry,' and 'have a nice day' about 10 times a day
Midwest nice except in Iowa they generally mean it
And now it is a Rally House because we didn't want a corner drugstore on Welch, apparently?

key_points: Career fair heavily skewed toward internships even for graduating seniors
ME grad with 2.98 GPA and one internship received 4 potential offers post-graduation
LinkedIn outperforms career fair for actual job interviews per multiple graduates
key_quotes: I found way more success applying through LinkedIn than I ever did going to the career fair
It was super difficult to get an internship... and now when it's finally time for a full time job ALL anyone is giving out is internships
Barely any on campus interviews, mostly just 'send us your resume' conversations

key_points: Outstanding value for in-state and merit scholarship recipients
Engineering and CS programs ranked competitively for cost vs. outcome
Campus bookstore and C-stores actively exploit captive student market
key_quotes: I will save over 100K in my college fund over the course of 4 years
Is a T20 education that good? Or am I crazy and just go to Iowa [State]
Iowa State bookstore, the biggest scam on campus

key_points: Strong alumni nostalgia — graduates consistently say they miss Ames
Community rallies around oddly specific campus legends (Campameal, Steve Butler, swans)
Political tensions rising on campus due to state-level legislative interference
key_quotes: I absolutely LOVE walking around campus and last night was perfect
I wish there was a way to know you're in the good old days before you've actually left them
It's heartbreaking to see people being punished for trying to support students

key_points: **Academic Integrity:** AI smart glasses used openly to cheat on exams with no enforcement
**Political Climate:** State legislation banning DEI topics and restricting faculty speech on campus
**Student Safety:** Documented ongoing harassment and cosplay club SA allegations with slow institutional response
key_quotes: This is the third year I've heard about creepy people in power in cosplay club, and I'm not in cosplay club. Admin really needs to check in there
Illegal to help gay students now. No free speech for faculty and staff. Iowa GOP outlawing free speech on campus
How are we at the point where we're using AI smart glasses to cheat on exams? I feel like I'm in a movie

key_points: Dr. Steve Butler posts free calc lecture videos and gives pre-exam Reddit pep talks
Hidden rooms across campus — abandoned labs, steam tunnels, stairs to nowhere in Friley
CyRide bus system is actively managed by a transparent, communicative operations manager
key_quotes: You are the LeBron of teaching, your videos helped me get a 99 in calc I
I got into the area under the Gilman lecture hall seats, that one was pretty cool. Probably the coolest was an abandoned lab in the attic of Coover
I'm Mike and I've been with CyRide for over a decade — I will generally post CyRide updates here each semester
